# elliptic

```go
import "crypto/elliptic"
```

Package elliptic implements the standard NIST P\-224, P\-256, P\-384, and P\-521 elliptic curves over prime fields.

Direct use of this package is deprecated, beyond the [P224](<#P224>), [P256](<#P256>), [P384](<#P384>), and [P521](<#P521>) values necessary to use [crypto/ecdsa](<https://pkg.go.dev/crypto/ecdsa/>). Most other uses should migrate to the more efficient and safer [crypto/ecdh](<https://pkg.go.dev/crypto/ecdh/>), or to third\-party modules for lower\-level functionality.

<a name="GenerateKey"></a>

## func [GenerateKey](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/crypto/elliptic/elliptic.go#L75>)

```go
func GenerateKey(curve Curve, rand io.Reader) (priv []byte, x, y *big.Int, err error)
```

GenerateKey returns a public/private key pair. The private key is generated using the given reader, which must return random data.

Deprecated: for ECDH, use the GenerateKey methods of the [crypto/ecdh](<https://pkg.go.dev/crypto/ecdh/>) package; for ECDSA, use the GenerateKey function of the crypto/ecdsa package.

<a name="Marshal"></a>

## func [Marshal](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/crypto/elliptic/elliptic.go#L109>)

```go
func Marshal(curve Curve, x, y *big.Int) []byte
```

Marshal converts a point on the curve into the uncompressed form specified in SEC 1, Version 2.0, Section 2.3.3. If the point is not on the curve \(or is the conventional point at infinity\), the behavior is undefined.

Deprecated: for ECDH, use the crypto/ecdh package. This function returns an encoding equivalent to that of PublicKey.Bytes in crypto/ecdh.

<a name="MarshalCompressed"></a>

## func [MarshalCompressed](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/crypto/elliptic/elliptic.go#L126>)

```go
func MarshalCompressed(curve Curve, x, y *big.Int) []byte
```

MarshalCompressed converts a point on the curve into the compressed form specified in SEC 1, Version 2.0, Section 2.3.3. If the point is not on the curve \(or is the conventional point at infinity\), the behavior is undefined.

<a name="Unmarshal"></a>

## func [Unmarshal](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/crypto/elliptic/elliptic.go#L153>)

```go
func Unmarshal(curve Curve, data []byte) (x, y *big.Int)
```

Unmarshal converts a point, serialized by [Marshal](<#Marshal>), into an x, y pair. It is an error if the point is not in uncompressed form, is not on the curve, or is the point at infinity. On error, x = nil.

Deprecated: for ECDH, use the crypto/ecdh package. This function accepts an encoding equivalent to that of the NewPublicKey methods in crypto/ecdh.

<a name="UnmarshalCompressed"></a>

## func [UnmarshalCompressed](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/crypto/elliptic/elliptic.go#L180>)

```go
func UnmarshalCompressed(curve Curve, data []byte) (x, y *big.Int)
```

UnmarshalCompressed converts a point, serialized by [MarshalCompressed](<#MarshalCompressed>), into an x, y pair. It is an error if the point is not in compressed form, is not on the curve, or is the point at infinity. On error, x = nil.

<a name="Curve"></a>

## type [Curve](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/crypto/elliptic/elliptic.go#L31-L66>)

A Curve represents a short\-form Weierstrass curve with a=\-3.

The behavior of Add, Double, and ScalarMult when the input is not a point on the curve is undefined.

Note that the conventional point at infinity \(0, 0\) is not considered on the curve, although it can be returned by Add, Double, ScalarMult, or ScalarBaseMult \(but not the [Unmarshal](<#Unmarshal>) or [UnmarshalCompressed](<#UnmarshalCompressed>) functions\).

Using Curve implementations besides those returned by [P224](<#P224>), [P256](<#P256>), [P384](<#P384>), and [P521](<#P521>) is deprecated.

```go
type Curve interface {
    // Params returns the parameters for the curve.
    Params() *CurveParams

    // IsOnCurve reports whether the given (x,y) lies on the curve.
    //
    // Deprecated: this is a low-level unsafe API. For ECDH, use the crypto/ecdh
    // package. The NewPublicKey methods of NIST curves in crypto/ecdh accept
    // the same encoding as the Unmarshal function, and perform on-curve checks.
    IsOnCurve(x, y *big.Int) bool

    // Add returns the sum of (x1,y1) and (x2,y2).
    //
    // Deprecated: this is a low-level unsafe API.
    Add(x1, y1, x2, y2 *big.Int) (x, y *big.Int)

    // Double returns 2*(x,y).
    //
    // Deprecated: this is a low-level unsafe API.
    Double(x1, y1 *big.Int) (x, y *big.Int)

    // ScalarMult returns k*(x,y) where k is an integer in big-endian form.
    //
    // Deprecated: this is a low-level unsafe API. For ECDH, use the crypto/ecdh
    // package. Most uses of ScalarMult can be replaced by a call to the ECDH
    // methods of NIST curves in crypto/ecdh.
    ScalarMult(x1, y1 *big.Int, k []byte) (x, y *big.Int)

    // ScalarBaseMult returns k*G, where G is the base point of the group
    // and k is an integer in big-endian form.
    //
    // Deprecated: this is a low-level unsafe API. For ECDH, use the crypto/ecdh
    // package. Most uses of ScalarBaseMult can be replaced by a call to the
    // PrivateKey.PublicKey method in crypto/ecdh.
    ScalarBaseMult(k []byte) (x, y *big.Int)
}
```

<a name="P224"></a>

### func [P224](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/crypto/elliptic/elliptic.go#L240>)

```go
func P224() Curve
```

P224 returns a [Curve](<#Curve>) which implements NIST P\-224 \(FIPS 186\-3, section D.2.2\), also known as secp224r1. The CurveParams.Name of this [Curve](<#Curve>) is "P\-224".

Multiple invocations of this function will return the same value, so it can be used for equality checks and switch statements.

The cryptographic operations are implemented using constant\-time algorithms.

<a name="P256"></a>

### func [P256](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/crypto/elliptic/elliptic.go#L253>)

```go
func P256() Curve
```

P256 returns a [Curve](<#Curve>) which implements NIST P\-256 \(FIPS 186\-3, section D.2.3\), also known as secp256r1 or prime256v1. The CurveParams.Name of this [Curve](<#Curve>) is "P\-256".

Multiple invocations of this function will return the same value, so it can be used for equality checks and switch statements.

The cryptographic operations are implemented using constant\-time algorithms.

<a name="P384"></a>

### func [P384](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/crypto/elliptic/elliptic.go#L265>)

```go
func P384() Curve
```

P384 returns a [Curve](<#Curve>) which implements NIST P\-384 \(FIPS 186\-3, section D.2.4\), also known as secp384r1. The CurveParams.Name of this [Curve](<#Curve>) is "P\-384".

Multiple invocations of this function will return the same value, so it can be used for equality checks and switch statements.

The cryptographic operations are implemented using constant\-time algorithms.

<a name="P521"></a>

### func [P521](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/crypto/elliptic/elliptic.go#L277>)

```go
func P521() Curve
```

P521 returns a [Curve](<#Curve>) which implements NIST P\-521 \(FIPS 186\-3, section D.2.5\), also known as secp521r1. The CurveParams.Name of this [Curve](<#Curve>) is "P\-521".

Multiple invocations of this function will return the same value, so it can be used for equality checks and switch statements.

The cryptographic operations are implemented using constant\-time algorithms.

<a name="CurveParams"></a>

## type [CurveParams](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/crypto/elliptic/params.go#L15-L22>)

CurveParams contains the parameters of an elliptic curve and also provides a generic, non\-constant time implementation of [Curve](<#Curve>).

The generic Curve implementation is deprecated, and using custom curves \(those not returned by [P224](<#P224>), [P256](<#P256>), [P384](<#P384>), and [P521](<#P521>)\) is not guaranteed to provide any security property.

```go
type CurveParams struct {
    P       *big.Int // the order of the underlying field
    N       *big.Int // the order of the base point
    B       *big.Int // the constant of the curve equation
    Gx, Gy  *big.Int // (x,y) of the base point
    BitSize int      // the size of the underlying field
    Name    string   // the canonical name of the curve
}
```

<a name="CurveParams.Add"></a>

### func \(\*CurveParams\) [Add](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/crypto/elliptic/params.go#L110>)

```go
func (curve *CurveParams) Add(x1, y1, x2, y2 *big.Int) (*big.Int, *big.Int)
```

Add implements Curve.Add.

Deprecated: the [CurveParams](<#CurveParams>) methods are deprecated and are not guaranteed to provide any security property. For ECDH, use the [crypto/ecdh](<https://pkg.go.dev/crypto/ecdh/>) package. For ECDSA, use the [crypto/ecdsa](<https://pkg.go.dev/crypto/ecdsa/>) package with a [Curve](<#Curve>) value returned directly from [P224](<#P224>), [P256](<#P256>), [P384](<#P384>), or [P521](<#P521>).
